meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project block. Learn how to build a SaaS pricing model and launch your first portfolio-ready app using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap is solved by focusing on utility over originality. Build a SaaS pricing calculator or tier management system. By mastering the logic behind recurring revenue models, you gain a high-value skill that every SaaS founder needs, instantly turning a common technical hurdle into a professional portfolio asset.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat in the void. You have the technical skills to build, but the 'what' remains elusive—a common paralysis that keeps brilliant developers from shipping. The secret isn't finding a world-changing idea; it is building the foundational infrastructure that every SaaS startup desperately needs: a robust, flexible pricing engine.

By building a pricing component, you move beyond simple 'To-Do' lists and into the realm of enterprise-grade logic. This isn't just code; it is the heartbeat of a business. Let’s transform your lack of ideas into a high-converting, production-ready SaaS module that proves you understand the intersection of engineering and revenue.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data structure for your pricing tiers—Free, Pro, and Enterprise—using clear JSON schema definitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model. Include fields for tier name, monthly cost, annual discount, feature limit arrays, and API rate limits.

steps.title: Initialize the Logic Controller
steps.description: Create the backend logic that calculates the difference between monthly and annual billing cycles, a core SaaS requirement.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript function that calculates annual savings percentage based on monthly vs yearly pricing inputs and returns a formatted currency string.

steps.title: Build the UI Component
steps.description: Develop a responsive pricing toggle switch that updates the UI state instantly without page reloads.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Tailwind CSS for a pricing toggle. Ensure smooth transitions between monthly and annual views.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe-Ready Payloads
steps.description: Prepare your data structure to be compatible with Stripe's API, ensuring your project is 'production-ready' for employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Transform our internal pricing model into a Stripe-compliant product creation payload format.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Run unit tests on your pricing logic to ensure no edge cases exist in your calculations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest tests covering boundary conditions: negative inputs, zero-cost tiers, and maximum integer limits.

pro_tips: Always separate your pricing logic from your UI; this makes it reusable for mobile apps or API-only services.
Use 'Price Anchoring' in your UI design—highlight the Pro plan as the 'Recommended' choice to show you understand CRO.
Avoid hardcoding currency; build your architecture to support multi-currency localization from day one.
Implement 'Feature Gating' logic early, as this is the primary reason SaaS apps need pricing tiers in the first place.
Treat your code as a product: document your API endpoints as if you were selling them to another developer.
content.next_steps: Once you have built the pricing engine, integrate it into a landing page template and deploy it to Vercel. Add the link to your GitHub profile to showcase your ability to build revenue-impacting features.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade component that solves a real business problem. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start shipping the infrastructure that companies pay to implement. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
